data.title: Top Quant rated communications equipment stocks as Nokia Oyj surges
data.description:
Nokia Oyj (NOK) rose higher on Monday morning after Bank of America upgraded the telecommunications infrastructure stock to a Buy rating from a prior ratings position of Neutral.

Shares of the company surged 8.93% on Monday, crossing the $10 mark for the first time this year and touching an intraday high of $10.44, a fresh 52-week high, against a previous close of $9.46.

In light of this, below is a list of the top 10 communications equipment stocks ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings.

Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE) leads the sector with a near-perfect Quant Rating of 4.99. Viasat, Inc. (VSAT) follows closely behind with a rating of 4.91, also earning a Strong Buy recommendation.

Other top-ranked companies include Ituran Location and Control Ltd. (ITRN), Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson (ERIC), and ADTRAN Holdings, Inc. (ADTN), each carrying Strong Buy or Buy ratings. The top of the list is dominated by stocks with bullish quantitative recommendations, while the lower half features Hold-rated names like Ciena Corporation (CIEN), Viavi Solutions Inc. (VIAV), and Ubiquiti Inc. (UI).

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings system grades stocks on their relative performance across critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any score of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating and any score of 2.5 or below considered a bearish rating.

Here is the list:

  1. Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE), Quant Rating: 4.99

  2. Viasat, Inc. (VSAT), Quant Rating: 4.91

  3. Ituran Location and Control Ltd. (ITRN), Quant Rating: 4.77

  4. Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson (publ) (ERIC), Quant Rating: 4.54

  5. ADTRAN Holdings, Inc. (ADTN), Quant Rating: 4.28

  6. NetScout Systems, Inc. (NTCT), Quant Rating: 3.75

  7. Ciena Corporation (CIEN), Quant Rating: 3.49

  8. Viavi Solutions Inc. (VIAV), Quant Rating: 3.49

  9. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. (AAOI), Quant Rating: 3.49

  10. Ubiquiti Inc. (UI), Quant Rating: 3.46

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL)

data.title: GE Vernova sued by Vineyard Wind in offshore wind dispute
data.description:
Vineyard Wind said it has filed a lawsuit against GE Vernova (GEV) in an attempt to block the wind turbine maker from abandoning work on its wind farm project off the coast of Massachusetts, Reuters reported late Friday.

Vineyard Wind, a ‌joint venture between Spain's Iberdrola (IBDRY) (IBDSF) and Denmark's Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners, filed a lawsuit in a Massachusetts state court last week accusing GE Vernova (GEV) of wrongly trying to terminate its contract at the ​wind project's "most vulnerable stage."

The 806 MW project in February became the ​first large-scale offshore wind farm in the U.S. to begin initial operations after federal judges allowed construction to resume on five major east coast ​projects that the Trump administration had halted near the end of ⁠2025.

But days later, GE Renewables US LLC (GEV) sent it a notice threatening to ​end its turbine supply agreement, the lawsuit said, citing a failure to pay amounts it was contractually due under a $1.3B contract.

Vineyard Wind said GE Vernova's (GEV) termination of the agreements was improper and has asked ​a judge to ​block the company from stopping ⁠work on the project, which "threatens to deprive hundreds of thousands of Massachusetts ratepayers of clean energy."

data.title: L3Harris picked for U.S. Army night-vision program under potential $465M contract
data.description:
L3Harris Technologies (LHX) was selected by the U.S. Army to supply its NOVA night-vision goggle system as part of the service’s Binocular Night Observation Device (BiNOD) program, under a contract that could be worth up to $465 million over seven years.

 

 

The company is one of several vendors chosen for the program, which is aimed at developing and fielding next-generation binocular night-vision capabilities for U.S. forces.

The NOVA system is a helmet-mounted device designed to provide situational awareness in low-light and adverse weather conditions. It incorporates image intensifier technology and is intended to support military operations over the long term.

The BiNOD effort builds on the Army’s existing night-vision modernization initiatives. L3Harris (LHX) has previously supplied the Enhanced Night Vision Goggle – Binocular (ENVG-B), with more than 20,000 units delivered to date.

Sen. Jeanne Shaheen said the investment supports both national security and economic activity, particularly in New Hampshire, where L3Harris (LHX) has operations.

Company executives said the contract follows collaboration with the Army to define requirements for a new binocular system, with production expected to begin following the award.

The Army hasn’t disclosed how many units will be procured under the BiNOD program or the timeline for fielding the new system.

data.title: Northrop Grumman, U.S. Air Force advance Sentinel ICBM program toward 2027 test
data.description:
Northrop Grumman (NOC) and the U.S. Air Force on Monday reported continued progress on the LGM-35A Sentinel intercontinental ballistic missile program, with development milestones achieved across testing, infrastructure and supply chain readiness as the effort moves toward a planned first flight in 2027 and initial operational capability in the early 2030s.

The Sentinel program is intended to replace the aging ground-based component of the U.S. nuclear triad, spanning more than 32,000 square miles across five states. The companies said the program is being developed using an incremental acquisition approach designed to accelerate deployment while allowing testing and validation to occur earlier in the process.

 

Development has progressed from digital design into hardware prototyping and early testing. Northrop Grumman (NOC) said it assembled an initial three-stage booster and completed testing on key propulsion components. Early flight-test hardware, including solid rocket motors for multiple planned test launches, is already in production.

Additional testing has focused on missile stage separation and payload protection systems, as well as guidance and control hardware. A sled test subjected navigation systems to simulated flight conditions to evaluate durability and performance under stress.

Beyond the missile itself, the program includes modernization of launch infrastructure. A prototype for a new modular launch silo is under development, intended to replace existing silos and reduce long-term maintenance and upgrade costs. Transport systems have also undergone cross-country testing to validate their ability to securely move sensitive components.

The program’s digital command-and-control element, known as the Launch Support System, has completed its critical design review and is moving into build and testing phases.

Program officials said further milestones are expected over the next several years, including continued prototyping, system validation and the first full flight test, as the system advances toward eventual deployment.

data.title: Scotiabank launches unified enterprise AI solution Scotia Intelligence
data.description:
Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS) (BNS:CA) announced on Monday the launch of a unified enterprise AI solution, Scotia Intelligence.

"Combined with our existing technology infrastructure, these AI capabilities unite the strength of our data and cloud environments while embedding governance and security so teams can use AI confidently and responsibly," said Tim Clark, head and chief information officer.

The bank said branches and contact centers were among the first to adopt purpose-built AI tools, and teams will continue to have access to new capabilities through Scotia Navigator.

Scotia Navigator, integrated into the bank's workplace to enhance day-to-day work, brings assistive AI capabilities for employees. For technical teams, Scotia Navigator provides advanced AI-powered coding assistance.

Scotia Intelligence can handle client queries, process commercial emails, and help clients to stay on top of routine banking tasks through timely and intuitive prompts.

data.title: RTX's Collins Aerospace wins contracts from Army’s next-generation tiltrotor
data.description:
Collins Aerospace, a unit of RTX (RTX), on Monday said it secured multiple contracts from Bell Textron to supply key systems for the U.S. Army’s Future Long Range Assault Aircraft, known as the MV-75 FLRAA.

The agreements cover five components for the next-generation rotorcraft program: main power generation, an interconnect drive system, the SmartProbe air data system, cockpit seating and an ice protection system. Collins said some of the work will be carried out using commercial acquisition authorities, a procurement approach intended to speed development and reduce costs.

 

 

The MV-75 FLRAA program, led by Bell, is a central part of the Army’s effort to modernize its aviation fleet. The aircraft is designed to replace portions of the current helicopter inventory with a platform capable of greater speed and range. The service has described it as its first clean-sheet rotorcraft design in decades.

Bell, a subsidiary of Textron (TXT), selected Collins Aerospace as a supplier as it continues to build out the industrial base supporting the program. The aircraft is being developed using digital engineering tools and a modular open systems architecture, which are intended to allow for upgrades and changes over time.

Work tied to the Collins contracts will be distributed across multiple U.S. locations, including facilities in Colorado, Illinois, Iowa, Minnesota, New York, Ohio and West Virginia.

The U.S. Army has positioned FLRAA as a cornerstone of its future vertical lift strategy, aiming to extend operational reach and improve mobility in contested environments.

data.title: National Grid sees slight earnings impact from U.S. regulation, storms
data.description:
National Grid (NGG) down 1.8% in Monday's trading after saying it is performing in line with prior guidance but estimates a net impact of ~1 pence/share to underlying earnings due to refund charges related to a recent regulatory ruling and storm costs in the U.S.

The impact reflects the recognition of customer refund charges related to the March judgment from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission on New England Transmission, as well as slightly higher than expected storm costs in its U.S. businesses, partly offset by slightly lower finance costs.

In November, National Grid (NGG) said growth in underlying EPS for the current financial year is expected in the 6%-8% range; in the financial year to March 2025, the utility reported underlying EPS of 73.3p.

data.title: Netflix wins sell-side praise for ad potential, content quality
data.description:
With the pursuit of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) in the rearview and a price hike successfully absorbed by subscribers, Netflix (NFLX) is now a lower-volatility business with a potential for “substantial” growth in its advertising business, leading Morgan Stanley and Wedbush to reiterate their bullish outlook on the stock with a corresponding hike to their respective price targets.

In a note to clients, Wedbush takes a closer look at Netflix’s (NFLX) advertising business and expects to see ad revenue to at least double to $3B this year, with “significant opportunities remaining in 2027 and beyond.”

“Netflix should continue to gain incrementally from its ad business by expanding partnerships, improving targeting, leveraging AI, and adding more live content,” notes Wedbush analyst Alicia Reese, with interactive ads enhancing its marketing capabilities.

Coupled with domestic subscription pricing, Reese remains positive on the company’s overall opportunity to expand revenue and reiterates her Outperform rating on Netflix (NFLX) with a 3% increase to her price target to $118.

Morgan Stanley’s Sean Diffley also sees additional ad revenue opportunities for Netflix (NFLX), while price hikes and an end to the Warner Bros. (WBD) pursuit also provide a tailwind to double-digit revenue growth.

Moreover, hours watched on Netflix are approaching 200B annually as quality of engagement is bolstered by live events and sporting events delivering outsized value and “must-see” moments.

“Nothing captures viewer attention more than live events, which carry urgency, unpredictability, and a sense of communal participation…the NFL is the pinnacle of this, so it is no surprise that Netflix is looking to expand its offering to 4 games from 2,” Diffley adds.

“Amidst concerns about the growth of absolute engagement, we see Netflix as the dominant player in the premium quality content space,” says Diffley, reiterating an Overweight rating on the stock with a 4.5% increase to his price target to $115.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors are also bullish on Netflix (NFLX) with a Buy consensus on the stock. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating views the stock as a Hold with a score of 3.25 out of 5, assigning low marks to Netflix’s current valuation.

data.title: Large-cap IT stocks with A+ grade EPS revisions as earnings season begins
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the Information Technology sector enters reporting season as one of the most closely watched, and highest-expectation, areas of the market.

This list ranks large-cap Information Technology stocks that have earned an A+ EPS revision grade from Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system. The screening criteria include stocks within the IT sector with large market capitalizations and the highest possible EPS revision grade.

The list includes Ciena Corporation (CIEN), a communications equipment company with a market cap of $70.14 billion. Coherent Corp. (COHR) and Dell Technologies Inc. (DELL) follow, with Dell notably carrying a Strong Buy Quant rating of 4.94 alongside its A+ EPS revision grade.

The list features a diverse range of IT industries, including communications equipment, semiconductors, electronic manufacturing services, and application software. All 12 companies maintain the highest A+ EPS revision grade, with two stocks—Dell Technologies Inc. (DELL) and TTM Technologies, Inc. (TTMI)—also earning Strong Buy Quant ratings.

  1. Ciena Corporation (CIEN) EPS revision grade: A+

  2. Coherent Corp. (COHR) EPS revision grade: A+

  3. Dell Technologies Inc. (DELL) EPS revision grade: A+

  4. F5, Inc. (FFIV) EPS revision grade: A+

  5. Flex Ltd. (FLEX) EPS revision grade: A+

  6. HubSpot, Inc. (HUBS) EPS revision grade: A+

  7. MKS Inc. (MKSI) EPS revision grade: A+

  8. Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (MPWR) EPS revision grade: A+

  9. Strategy Inc (MSTR) EPS revision grade: A+

  10. MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings, Inc. (MTSI) EPS revision grade: A+

  11. TD SYNNEX Corporation (SNX) EPS revision grade: A+

  12. TTM Technologies, Inc. (TTMI) EPS revision grade: A+

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Amazon expands its auto business by adding mainstream brands
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) significantly expanded the Amazon Autos business by adding major new brands and rolling out to more cities. The expansion marks a substantial escalation of its bet on the automotive retail market. The e-commerce giant has quietly broadened its platform over the last 18 months, moving beyond its initial exclusive partnership with Hyundai (HYMLF) to now include vehicles from Jeep (STLA), Chevrolet (GM), Kia (HYMLF), Mazda (MZDAY), and Subaru (FUJHY) (FUJHF). 

Notably, the added auto brands transform Amazon Autos from a limited pilot into a comprehensive multi-brand marketplace, allowing customers to purchase brand-new cars from a much wider selection of manufacturers directly through the Amazon website. The growth strategy focuses on both inventory depth and geographic reach. While the service launched in late 2024 in 48 cities, the addition of mainstream U.S. and Asian brands significantly increases the addressable market for potential buyers. 

The Amazon Autos platform continues to operate on a marketplace model where local dealerships list their inventory, set haggle-free upfront prices, and maintain the customer relationship for final pickup and delivery, while Amazon facilitates the browsing, financing, and checkout process.

Looking ahead, the aggressive move positions Amazon (AMZN) to compete more directly with established online car retailers and traditional digital listing sites, leveraging its massive Prime member base and trusted checkout infrastructure to potentially capture a larger share of the car-buying market.

Related auto retail tickers: Carvana (CVNA), CarMax (KMX), Lithia Motors (LAD), AutoNation (AN), Asbury Automotive Group (ABG), CarGurus (CARG), Group 1 Automotive (GPI), Sonic Automotive (SAH), Penske Automotive Group (PAG), Vroom (VRM), Shift Technologies (SFTGQ), Rush Enterprise (RUSHA), Driven Brands (DRVN), Openlane (OPLN), ACV Auctions (ACVA), Cars.com (CARS), Tesla (TSLA), Toyota Motor (TM), Honda Motor (HMC), Nissan (NSANY), General Motors (GM), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Lucid Group (LCID), Stellantis (STLA), and Ford Motor (F).
